query: Browser agent that’s 10x faster than Claude review

results.title: Pluno: 10x faster than Claude in Browser | Product Hunt
results.content:
Hey makers!  
  
Here’s what Claude & other browser agents got wrong:  
They use software like humans.  
  
They wait for pages.  
They stare at screenshots.  
They click buttons.  
They get stuck in dropdowns.  
  
You’ve probably seen this - impressive for a demo, painful when you actually need work done.  
  
So we built Pluno.  
  
Pluno skips the UI entirely and talks directly to the APIs underneath your web apps. You tell it the outcome, and it gets the task done in the background.  
  
No clicks.  
No dropdowns.  
No babysitting.  
  
Just: Done ✅  
  
You can use Pluno as a browser extension to update records, extract data, fill forms, run bulk actions, create dashboards, and handle annoying SaaS tasks that normally eat up your day. [...] Pluno

AwesomeQA [...] We benchmarked Pluno across 312 real-world tasks in 24 tools (incl. HubSpot, Notion, Stripe, Linear), and the results speak for themselves: 34% higher success rate than Claude’s browser extension, at ~14x the speed.  
  
Why we’ve built it? Because we loved the idea of Claude’s browser extension, but in reality it was way too slow to be useful.  
  
For founders, operators, and tiny teams doing the work of ten people:   
This one is for you 💜  
  
What I’d love to know: Which browser task would you use to test Pluno’s limits?  
  
P.S. Get 50$ in free credits, today only.

results.title: Claude Code Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
5.0

Based on 538 reviews

Review Claude Code?

Reviewers say Claude Code stands out for deep codebase understanding, strong reasoning, and agentic workflows that can handle multi-step, multi-file tasks in the terminal, often with less hand-holding than Cursor, Copilot, or browser chat tools. Users and founders alike say it helps ship faster on real products, from refactors and debugging to full features, while fitting naturally into existing workflows. The main complaints are a real learning curve, occasional friction with permissions or tool behavior, weaker frontend and edge-case support, and concerns about visibility into spend.

Claude Code CLI is already seamless and consistently delivers high-quality results. The main area for improvement would be deeper scalability toward a full agentic development environment (ADE), similar to what tools like Warp are evolving toward—bringing more autonomous workflows, richer context management, and tighter developer-environment integration.

results.title: Claude by Anthropic Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
#### What needs improvement

limited image output (2)

The limitations on image output and image generation are the only thing I can think of

#### vs Alternatives

ChatGPT by OpenAIChatGPT by OpenAIGeminiGemini

For marketing, nothing even comes close to the output quality of Claude. For writing, I believe it's a combination of solid context awareness and just better, more natural writing skills. But the real game-changer for productivity is Claude Cowork (and eventually Claude Code, I would think, but so far Cowork is what I use) as a desktop agent with local access. Gemini doesn't have that, OpenAI's option seems much more limited and less user-friendly

Report

 72 views

•1 review

#### What's great [...] Based on 873 reviews

Review Claude by Anthropic?

Reviewers mostly see Claude as a strong tool for coding, long-form reasoning, and writing that sounds natural instead of generic. They repeatedly praise its ability to keep context across long chats, understand large codebases and documents, explain clearly, and sometimes push back rather than bluff. Founders of products like UXPin, IFTTT, and Wordware also highlight its production-ready output and agent workflows. Common complaints are strict message limits, weak image features, occasional refusals or hallucinations, and some rough app or UX issues.

#### What's great [...] When building autonomous agent workflows (like the ones my project, Vela, secures and sandboxes), the biggest bottleneck isn't just raw intelligence—it's reliability and instruction following. While platforms like Flowise or GPTBots are great for orchestrating pipelines, and DeepSeek is fantastic for raw code generation, Claude by Anthropic is in a league of its own when it comes to agentic behavior. When you give an LLM the ability to write and execute code, you need a model that strictly adheres to system prompts, outputs perfectly formatted JSON for tool calls, and doesn't hallucinate mid-reasoning. Claude consistently respects constraints and boundaries better than the alternatives. It feels less like a chatbot and more like a reliable junior engineer that actually reads the [...] Subscribe

results.content:

automations (1)browser is laggy (1)

 The automations don't work when the VPS is "sleeping". So an automation every day at 7 AM doesn't work, but at noon it works since the chances that it's still awake are high
 It's cool to be able to view Claude's browser, but it's too laggy to be really useful
